title: Quiz: The Hadamard Gate
description: Test your understanding of the Hadamard gate and its properties

questions.question: What is H|0⟩, where H is the Hadamard gate?
questions.explanation: The Hadamard gate transforms |0⟩ into an equal superposition: H|0⟩ = (|0⟩ + |1⟩)/√2 = |+⟩.

questions.question: What is H|1⟩?
questions.explanation: H|1⟩ = (|0⟩ - |1⟩)/√2 = |-⟩. Note the minus sign compared to H|0⟩.

questions.question: The Hadamard gate is its own inverse: H² = I.
questions.explanation: True! H² = HH = I. This means applying the Hadamard gate twice returns the qubit to its original state.

questions.question: What is H|+⟩, where |+⟩ = (|0⟩ + |1⟩)/√2?
questions.explanation: Since H² = I and H|0⟩ = |+⟩, we have H|+⟩ = H(H|0⟩) = H²|0⟩ = |0⟩. The Hadamard converts between computational and Hadamard bases.

questions.question: What is HZH, where Z is the Pauli Z gate?
questions.explanation: HZH = X. The Hadamard gate conjugates Z to X (and X to Z). This is because H swaps the computational (Z) and Hadamard (X) bases.

questions.question: After applying H to |0⟩, what is the probability of measuring |0⟩?
questions.explanation: H|0⟩ = (|0⟩ + |1⟩)/√2. The probability of measuring |0⟩ is |1/√2|² = 1/2 = 0.5 (50%).

questions.question: The eigenvalues of the Hadamard gate are:
questions.explanation: The Hadamard gate has eigenvalues +1 and -1. This can be verified by computing det(H - λI) = 0.
